raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need to echo date and time then " 1". Append to file. Use date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' maybe. Command: echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') 1" >> /system/folder3/temp/empty.txt. Also output to console: use tee -a. So: echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') 1" | tee -a /system/folder3/temp/empty.txt. That writes to console and file. Provide one command.
